result.summary: Google Cloud Build exposes a REST API at `https://cloudbuild.googleapis.com` and a `gcloud builds` CLI surface; both use Google Cloud authentication via OAuth/ADC-backed bearer credentials.
result.description: Google Cloud Build is a managed CI/CD service on Google Cloud for running builds, tests, and deployments. It integrates with source repositories and Google Cloud deployment targets to automate software delivery workflows.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud OAuth / Application Default Credentials
gcp_oauth.setup: For REST or client-library access, create Google Cloud credentials in [APIs & Services > Credentials](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials) or use Application Default Credentials as documented in [Set up ADC for a local development environment](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/set-up-adc-local-dev-environment). For command-line use, install the [gcloud CLI](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install-sdk) and sign in with `gcloud auth login` or initialize with `gcloud init`; Google Cloud documentation lists these under [Authenticate for the gcloud CLI](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/authenticate) and [Authenticate for using REST](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/rest).

variables.name: projectId
variables.description: Google Cloud project ID used in resource paths like `/v1/projects/{projectId}/builds`.

variables.name: location
variables.description: Some Cloud Build resources are regional and use a `{location}` path segment under `/v1/projects/{projectId}/locations/{location}/...`.
